title: X-Light Norwegian Fish Stew with Speculaas Spice Mix
description: A light and cozy stew recipe from Norway that uses fermented fish and speculaas spice mix. Perfect for a low-carb weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Speculaas Spice Mix
ingredients.notes: Available at most spice stores or online.

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a clay pot over medium heat.
Add the thinly sliced onion, carrots, and celery and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, fish stock, speculaas spice mix, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Stir in the beaten egg yolk to thicken the stew.
Add the fermented cod pieces and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until heated through.
Stir in the chopped fresh dill and heavy cream.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional fresh dill if desired.
Let the stew cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
To reheat, place the stew in the clay pot and cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
Serve with a side of rye bread or over cauliflower rice for a low-carb option.
If desired, serve with a dollop of sour cream or a sprinkle of chopped chives.
